title: podman images --filter  reference=<repo> prints out matches from other repos
body:
### Issue Description

This is a continuation of a similar issue described in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25725 and "fixed" in https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2413 (after library merge look at https://github.com/containers/container-libs/commit/74e3753df374ee659fc2434c5eab63d03a761dbc). Note I put "fixed" in quotes here because the reproducer described in the original issue is exactly still reproducible today.

Here's the crux of the problem:

```
[bound] -bash-5.3$ podman pull --quiet quay.io/fedora/fedora:44                                     
113f8751d76ff94ef19b09a3523e617018b6924a3f5b5941e99ee5d897941aee                                    
[bound] -bash-5.3$ podman tag quay.io/fedora/fedora:44 localhost/fedora-local:latest                
[bound] -bash-5.3$ podman images --format '{{.Repository}} {{.Tag}}' --noheading --filter  reference='localhost/fedora-local'                                                                            
localhost/fedora-local latest 
quay.io/fedora/fedora 44                                                                            
[bound] -bash-5.3$
```

So we end up with two pieces of output when we were asking for information about only `--filter  reference='localhost/fedora-local'`.

```
[bound] -bash-5.3$ podman images --format '{{.Id}}' --noheading --filter  reference='localhost/fedora-local'                                                                                             
113f8751d76ff94ef19b09a3523e617018b6924a3f5b5941e99ee5d897941aee                                                                                                                                         
113f8751d76ff94ef19b09a3523e617018b6924a3f5b5941e99ee5d897941aee
```
                                               


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pull --quiet quay.io/fedora/fedora:44`
2. `podman tag quay.io/fedora/fedora:44 localhost/fedora-local:latest`
3. `podman images --format '{{.Repository}} {{.Tag}}' --noheading --filter  reference='localhost/fedora-local'`


### Describe the results you received

Images from `quay.io/fedora/fedora` in the output

### Describe the results you expected

Expected only images matching `reference='localhost/fedora-local'` to be output.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

```
[bound] -bash-5.3$ rpm -q podman                                                                    
podman-5.8.1-1.fc44.x86_64
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: This is expected behavior per the design in https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2413. Name filtering only applies when the reference is **fully qualified** (includes a tag or digest). `localhost/fedora-local` is a bare repo name without a tag, so the filter matches the image but does not restrict which names are displayed. Using `--filter reference='localhost/fedora-local:latest'` will return only the matching entry.

Thanks for the reply @Honny1.

Is it expected that the reproducer in the description of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25725 is exactly reproducible today?

```
[core@cosa-devsh ~]$ mkdir build
[core@cosa-devsh ~]$ cd build/
[core@cosa-devsh build]$ echo "FROM fedora:41" > Containerfile
[core@cosa-devsh build]$ podman build -t example
Error: no context directory and no Containerfile specified
[core@cosa-devsh build]$ podman build -t example .
STEP 1/1: FROM fedora:41
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:41...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b2e5d4693765 done   | 
Copying config 432fb3cbc4 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
COMMIT example
--> 432fb3cbc49c
Successfully tagged localhost/example:latest
Successfully tagged registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:41
432fb3cbc49c04402a3dbf71c18aab8e302fe31c9697762f9ec4718423ed70b4
[core@cosa-devsh build]$ podman images example
REPOSITORY                         TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
localhost/example                  latest      432fb3cbc49c  4 months ago  167 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  41          432fb3cbc49c  4 months ago  167 MB
```

I just don't understand why you would use `podman images --filter reference='localhost/fedora-local:latest'`. If you know the fully qualified name why not just use `podman inspect localhost/fedora-local:latest`?

The reference filter operates on images, not on individual names. Since `localhost/fedora-local` (without a tag) doesn't unambiguously identify a single name, Podman finds the matching image and displays all its tags. Your example shows one image with two tags (`localhost/fedora-local:latest` and `quay.io/fedora/fedora:44`), and both are valid names for that same image.

If you need an exact lookup, `podman inspect` or `--filter reference='localhost/fedora-local:latest'` (with the tag) will give you a single result. For example, `podman inspect` shows it is indeed one image:
``` bash
$ podman pull --quiet quay.io/fedora/fedora:44
722f849805f3cd4b446c5244ef4f82f60cdeeda37879e816fad9721c2f684546
$ podman tag quay.io/fedora/fedora:44 localhost/fedora-local:latest
$ podman inspect localhost/fedora-local
[
     {
          "Id": "722f849805f3cd4b446c5244ef4f82f60cdeeda37879e816fad9721c2f684546",
          ...
          "RepoTags": [
               "localhost/fedora-local:latest",
               "quay.io/fedora/fedora:44"
          ],
          ...
     }
]
```

Both `localhost/fedora-local:latest` and `quay.io/fedora/fedora:44` are names for the same image (`722f849805f3`), so when filtering by a bare repo name, Podman correctly lists all of them.

Thank you for the explanation. I can't say I fully understand the rationale, but trust you that it's working as designed.

Can you (I'm not allowed since the conversation is locked) add a comment to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25725 to state that the specific problem documented in the description wasn't fixed by https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2413 and is actually the intended behavior.
